questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: On $R$, the set of real numbers, a relation $\rho$ is defined as $a \rho b$ if and only if $1+a b>0$. Then,
options: $\rho$ is reflexive and transitive but not symmetric
$\rho$ is reflexive and symmetric but not transitive
questions.solution:
We know that,
$1+a^{2}>0, a \in R$
$\Rightarrow \quad(a, a) \in \rho$
$\therefore \rho$ is reflexive
Again, $\quad$ let $(a, b) \in \rho$
$\Rightarrow \quad 1+a b>0$
$\Rightarrow \quad 1+b a>0$
$\Rightarrow \quad(b, a) \in \rho$
$\therefore \rho$ is symmetric Now, $\quad(1,-0 \cdot 1) \in \rho$
and $(-0 \cdot 1,-9) \in \rho$
but $\quad(1,-9) \notin \rho$
$\therefore \rho$ is not transitive.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: If \(R\) and \(\mathrm{R}^1\) are equivalence relations on a set \(A\), then so are the relation
questions.solution:
Hint: If \(R ~\&~ R^1\) are equivalence relation

\(\mathrm{R}^{-1} \Rightarrow\) Equivalence

\(\mathrm{R} \cap \mathrm{R}^1 \Rightarrow\) Equivalence

\(R \cup R^1\) may or may not equivalence

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A relation $\rho$ on the set of real number $R$ is defined as $\{x \rho y: x y>0\} .$ Then, which of the following is/are true?
questions.solution:
We have, $x p y: x y>0$
(i) Reflexive Suppose $x \rho x \in R$
$\Rightarrow$
$x^{2} > 0$
which is not true when $x=0$. Hence, relation is not reflexive.
(ii) Symmetric $x p y \in R$ $\Rightarrow \quad x y>0$
$\Rightarrow \quad y x>0$
$\Rightarrow \quad y \rho x \in R$
If $(x, y) \in R,$ then, relation is symmetric.
(iii) Transitive $\mathrm{xp} \mathrm{y} \in \mathrm{R}$
$x y>0$
and $\quad y \rho z \in R$ $y z>0$
$\begin{array}{lc}
\Rightarrow & x y^{2} z>0 \\
\Rightarrow & x z>0 \\
\Rightarrow & (x, z) \in R \\
\text { If }(x, y) \in R, \text { then }(y, z) \in R \\
\Rightarrow & (x, z) \in R
\end{array}$
Hence, relation is transitive.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
